input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: Both .NET 9 and 10 runtimes are available. Let me read the source files.

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to create a BenchmarkDotNet project that benchmarks AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount, targeting both net9.0 and net10.0. BenchmarkDotNet supports multi-TFM benchmarking with the `--runtimes` flag or by setting up the project to target multiple frameworks.

Let me create a benchmark project that:
1. References the TextAnalyzer code
2. Uses BenchmarkDotNet
3. Targets both net9.0 and net10.0

I'll create a separate benchmark project that multi-targets net9.0 and net10.0.

toolCalls.name: create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-e6d7f257dca0485aa308b68d8beddac1/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj b/tmp/sv-e6d7f257dca0485aa308b68d8beddac1/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-e6d7f257dca0485aa308b68d8beddac1/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj
@@ -1,0 +1,15 @@
+<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
+  <PropertyGroup>
+    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
+    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
+    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
+    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
+  </PropertyGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.0" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <Compile Include="../TextAnalyzer/TextAnalyzer.cs" Link="TextAnalyzer.cs" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+</Project>
+

data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 doesn't have Net100 moniker yet. Let me check what version of BenchmarkDotNet supports .NET 10, or use a nightly/prerelease version. Alternatively, I can try a different approach:

1. Use `BenchmarkDotNet.Environments.CoreRuntime.CreateForNewVersion("net10.0", ".NET 10.0")` 
2. Use a newer version of BenchmarkDotNet
3. Use a manual benchmarking approach

Let me check if there's a newer version of BenchmarkDotNet available that supports .NET 10.

toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet nuget search BenchmarkDotNet --take 1 2>&1 | head -10", "description": "Check latest BenchmarkDotNet version"}
